{{ idnText }}

Integration time (NPLC)
{{ n.label }}
{{ n.plc }}
{{ nplcNote }}
{{ azLabel }}
{{ sndLabel }}
How readings are taken
Each test point sets the meter function explicitly (:FUNC:… + autorange), waits for autorange to settle, and discards the first readings — so no stale value or false OVERLOAD carries over from the previous point.
OL from the meter means open circuit (probes not in contact — normal between points in Ω / continuity / diode modes) or over range (reading beyond the range in V / A modes). BenchLink treats OL as "waiting for contact": it never fails a board, never enters the plot, and never gets captured.
Capture requires the reading to be near the point's expected band and hold steady for the hold time — so touching the wrong pad or a drifting contact won't record.
{{ miniLabel }}
{{ miniLast }}
open live plot →
